original_abstract: Due to its low storage cost and fast query speed, hashing has been widely adopted for similarity search in multimedia data. In particular, more and more attentions have been payed to multimodal hashing for search in multimedia data with multiple modalities, such as images with tags. Typically, supervised information of semantic labels is also available for the data points in many real applications. Hence, many supervised multimodal hashing~(SMH) methods have been proposed to utilize such semantic labels to further improve the search accuracy. However, the training time complexity of most existing SMH methods is too high, which makes them unscalable to large-scale datasets. In this paper, a novel SMH method, called semantic correlation maximization~(SCM), is proposed to seamlessly integrate semantic labels into the hashing learning procedure for large-scale data modeling. Experimental results on two real-world datasets show that SCM can significantly outperform the state-of-the-art SMH methods, in terms of both accuracy and scalability.
humanized_abstract: Due to its low storage cost and fast query speed, hashing has been widely used for similarity search in multimedia data. With the increasing number of modalities of multimedia data such as images with tags, multimodal hashing has become a new research trend for effective search in multimedia data. In many real-world applications, the supervised information of semantic labels for the data points are also available. Therefore, a large number of supervised multimodal hashing (SMH) methods have been proposed in order to exploit the semantic labels to further improve the search performance. Unfortunately, almost all existing SMH methods suffer from long training time complexity, which is not suitable for large-scale datasets. To address the issue, in this paper, we propose a novel SMH method, called Semantic Correlation Maximization (SCM), which integrates the semantic labels into the hashing learning process and thus enables fast and efficient large-scale data modeling. The experimental results on two large-scale real-world datasets have shown that our proposed SCM method not only achieves the state-of-the-art search performance but also exhibits favorable time efficiency when compared with the state-of-the-art SMH methods.

In many real-world applications, the supervised information of semantic labels for the data points are also available. Therefore, a large number of supervised multimodal hashing (SMH) methods have been proposed in order to exploit the semantic labels to further improve the search performance. Unfortunately, almost all existing SMH methods suffer from long training time complexity, which is not suitable for large-scale datasets. To address the issue, in this paper, we propose a novel SMH method, called Semantic Correlation Maximization (SCM), which integrates the semantic labels into the hashing learning process and thus enables fast and efficient large-scale data modeling. The experimental results on two large-scale real-world datasets have shown that our proposed SCM method not only achieves the state-of-the-art search performance but also exhibits favorable time efficiency when compared with the state-of-the-art SMH methods.
